commit.message:
Redesign with Clawless-inspired theme + add Schedules and Memory

Design overhaul:
- New color scheme: dark blue-black (#060a14) with teal/cyan accent
  (#00c8e0) and emerald secondary (#20dfa0), inspired by Clawless
  Command Deck theme but with its own identity
- Gradient accent buttons and logo (cyan to emerald)
- Subtle glow effects on cards, pulsing status dots
- Gradient text for Pulse branding
- Updated all components to use new design tokens

New features (inspired by OpenClaw AI team video):
- Schedules: Cron-like automated agent runs on intervals (30m to daily)
  with enable/disable, run-now, and auto-execution via server-side timer
- Memory: Per-agent knowledge files (.md) with file tree sidebar and
  built-in editor for storing rules, prompts, and context
- Agent roles: developer, reviewer, researcher, writer, tester, devops,
  designer, general — shown as badges on agent cards
- Cost estimation: Real-time estimated cost per agent based on model
  pricing, shown in stats bar and per-agent cards
- 5 stats in header: Active, Total, Tokens, Est. Cost, Errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: fix JS tests for async links/apiFetch; add PHP tests for new features

- Add @wordpress/api-fetch virtual mock; seed apiFetch.mockResolvedValue([])
  in beforeEach so findExistingTagsForThemes resolves without network calls
- getActiveLinkMap now mockResolvedValue([]) (async); all IA Links tests
  converted to async + await act(async () => { fireEvent.click() })

  pass the count > 30 threshold and dispatch/openGeneralSidebar are called

- Add writeToElement stub to aiHelper mock

PHP (bootstrap.php):

- Add WP_REST_Server stub (READABLE constant)

PHP (AiPostAssistantTest.php):
- 4 tests for sanitize_resumo_max_length (positive, zero, negative, non-numeric)
- 3 tests for rest_get_link_map (empty, valid JSON, invalid JSON)

commit.message:
Add rectangular selection to Classify, Similar, and Person faces views

Drag on any face grid to draw a rubber-band rectangle; all face
thumbnails that intersect it become selected.  Click behaviour is
unchanged (drag threshold is 6 px).

attachRectSelect(gridEl, cb) is a shared utility that:
- shows a .rect-select overlay while dragging
- suppresses the post-mouseup click event so individual-face click
  handlers don't fire after a drag
- returns a cleanup function that removes listeners and is wired into
  _cleanup so it is torn down on navigation

Add Rule 10: ANTICIPATE with evidence vs inference guardrail

Rule 10 ANTICIPATE:
- Think one step ahead, answer next question before asked
- Confidence ≥91% + evidence → state it, act
- Confidence ≥91% + inference → state + flag "inference, not verified"
- Confidence <91% → options + recommendation

Protocol Quick Reference updated:
- Added ANTICIPATE block with confidence thresholds

Anti-pattern table updated:
- Added "Confident Guess" anti-pattern
- Correction: ANTICIPATE guardrail — flag evidence vs inference
- Source: REPORTS-001

fix: add rounded corners to macOS icons for DMG volume consistency

but not to DMG volume icons on the desktop. Pre-baking rounded
corners (~22% radius) into the macOS PNGs and .icns fixes this.
Windows .ico and Store icons are unchanged.

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-06-S26: THM-052 PROVED — circulant tournaments have scalar M=(H/n)*I at odd n

New theorem with complete algebraic proof:
- Reflection-reversal bijection φ gives palindromic N(d,j)
- Palindromic + even length => zero alternating sum => off-diagonal M = 0
- Verified exhaustively at n=3,5,7 and spot-checked n=9
- Applies to all circulant, Paley, and doubly regular tournaments

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): bump to v0.3.1 - remove unsupported template var from .mcp.json

${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} is a runtime env var, not a .mcp.json template
variable. Only ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is supported for template expansion.
The MCP server reads CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR from process.env at runtime.

commit.message:
Fix CI: use wrangler deploy --no-bundle to skip OpenNext re-invocation

Wrangler detects the OpenNext project and spawns opennextjs-cloudflare
deploy, which creates a nested wrangler process that loses the
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN env var. Since build:cf already produces the
.open-next/ output, use --no-bundle to deploy the pre-built worker
directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 1: Identity bridge — ACT chain client, registration, trust sync

- New act_chain_client.py: HTTP client to ACT REST API (urllib, zero deps)
- sage_web4_lct_bridge.py: register_on_chain() mints LCT on ACT chain
- trust_tensor_sync.py: ChainBackedTrustSync settles T3 at session boundary
- sage_daemon.py: chain registration on boot + trust sync on shutdown (non-blocking)
- machine_config.py: act_chain_url field on all 7 machines (ACT_CHAIN_URL env override)
- identity.json seed: web4_lct_id field (null until registered)

All chain operations are non-blocking with graceful fallback.
SAGE boots and runs normally when ACT chain is unreachable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v7.9: fix TG white screen — give .app definite height instead of min-height

so content-area flex:1 properly fills the space.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix API route to match actual ctfpl_matches schema

Columns were completely wrong. Actual DB uses:
- team_a_name/team_b_name (not squad_a_name/squad_b_name)
- team_a_result/team_b_result (Win/Loss, not scores)
- team_a_kills/team_b_kills (for score values)
- match_date (not played_at)
- match_type (not status/title)
- mvp_player_name (not mvp)
- game_length_minutes (numeric, not match_length text)
- team_a_squad_id/team_b_squad_id

GET now transforms DB rows to frontend format.
POST inserts with correct column names.
Match length input accepts "22:45" (mm:ss) or "22" (minutes).

  Outro(아르페지오→페이드아웃)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WebSocket proxy traversal to reflector (#79)

* Clean up relay reflector modes and add WebSocket proxy support

This PR cleans up the relay reflector mode logic and adds WebSocket
proxy support:

Reflector Modes:
- disabled: no reflector at all
- registration: only reflect broker server URL (not accept file origins)
- traffic: only reflect accept file origins (not broker server URL) - DEFAULT
- all: reflect both broker server URL and accept file origins

Changes:
- Add helper methods to RelayReflectorMode (IsEnabled, ReflectsRegistration,
  ReflectsTraffic) for cleaner mode checking
- Fix bug where broker server URL was always routed through reflector
  regardless of mode - now correctly checks ReflectsRegistration()
- Add WebSocket upgrade detection and tunnel proxy to reflector
- Add REFLECTOR_WEBSOCKET_UPGRADE env var (default: true) to control
  WebSocket support
- Add comprehensive unit tests for mode helpers and WebSocket proxy
- Add e2e test validation for WebSocket mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add WebSocket proxy traversal support to reflector

Enable the reflector to handle WebSocket upgrade requests by tunneling
raw TCP between client and target connections. Fix unit test assertion
for connection-refused case and update e2e test to verify WebSocket
transport via broker server logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Upgrade to broker with fixed ws ref

* Add relay idle timeout to restart broker when no traffic flows

When the broker's connection goes stale (e.g., WebSocket connect/delete
cycle), no relay traffic flows through the reflector. This adds a
configurable idle timeout (RELAY_IDLE_TIMEOUT, default 5m) that restarts
the broker when no traffic has been seen, providing automatic recovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Gate idle timeout watchdog on reflector traffic mode

Only start the idle timeout goroutine when the reflector mode includes
traffic (traffic, all). In registration-only mode, no traffic flows
through the reflector so the watchdog would constantly restart.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
